originalTitle: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
title: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
content: Cameroon's President Paul Biya, 93, won his eighth successive term last year in a disputed election.

title: Senegal bans ministers from foreign travel as oil price rise bites
content: The fuel cost is nearly double what the government budgeted for putting pressure on stretched finances.

content: People evicted to build Nigeria's capital accuse the government of failing to fulfill its promises to them.

originalTitle: Is war more profitable than peace? David Keen explains
title: Is war more profitable than peace? David Keen explains
content: A leading political economist breaks down how war can reward power and be beneficial for profits and political survival.

originalTitle: Dozens missing after boat carrying migrants capsizes near Libya
title: Dozens missing after boat carrying migrants capsizes near Libya
content: Dozens of people are missing and at least two confirmed dead after a boat carrying migrants capsized off coast of Libya.

content: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

title: Analysis: Trump declares victory in Iran war after rescue, but threats to US operation still loom
content: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

originalTitle: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
title: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
content: The incident comes a week before the polls, and follow warnings of a potential operations staged to influence voters.

content: Leeds United will play Chelsea at Wembley after reaching the FA Cup semi-finals for the first time in almost four decades, while Manchester City take on Southampton.

title: Liverpool 'gave up' in FA Cup exit - Van Dijk
content: Liverpool captain Virgil van Dijk apologises to the supporters and says his side "gave up" during their 4-0 FA Cup quarter-final defeat by Manchester City.
